title: 📉 Enterprise Lagging Indicators [Edu]
description: Enterprise-level ESSP lagging indicators — DORA outcomes (deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, mean time to recovery) plus PR throughput per active developer. Pairs with the 🏢 Enterprise Copilot Leading Indicators dashboard. API-derivable metrics only — survey- and finance-based ESSP metrics are intentionally excluded.

options.content:
# 📉 Enterprise Lagging Indicators

## Why lagging indicators?

The [GitHub Engineering System Success Playbook (ESSP)](https://github.com/resources/insights/engineering-system-success-playbook) recommends balancing **leading** and **lagging** indicators to drive engineering system improvements ([ESSP e-book, *Leading versus lagging metrics*](https://assets.ctfassets.net/wfutmusr1t3h/59IWCIRvx0KfHbh6B7bv62/d9f55b94ab43fe91e9ed183d73882954/2025-05-28-GitHub-ESSP-Ebook-EZ-Version012.pdf)):

- **Lagging indicators** — outcomes measured *after* work is completed (deployment frequency, MTTR, change failure rate). Important for long-term results, but gains take time to be realized.
- **Leading indicators** — early signals close to the source of friction (PR review activity, Copilot engagement). Surfaced on the companion [🏢 Enterprise Copilot Leading Indicators](/d/edu-enterprise-leading) dashboard.

## What this dashboard shows

This dashboard surfaces **enterprise-scoped lagging metrics** for the four DORA pillars plus a Copilot-API-derived productivity ratio. Each section's metric is paired with the companion *leading* metric on dashboard 10 — so you can see, for example, whether rising Copilot review engagement (leading) is followed by improving lead time (lagging).

| Lagging indicator (this dashboard) | ESSP / DORA category | Leading companion (dashboard 10) |
|---|---|---|
| Deployment frequency | DORA Velocity / ESSP Activity | PRs created (`total_created`, `total_created_by_copilot`) |
| Lead time for changes (P50, P90 — merge → deploy) | DORA Velocity / ESSP Efficiency & Flow | Median minutes to merge |
| Change failure rate + rework rate | DORA Quality / ESSP Performance | Active vs passive Copilot review engagement |
| Failed deployment recovery time / MTTR | DORA Quality / ESSP Efficiency & Flow | Suggestion apply rate |
| PRs merged per active developer | ESSP Activity | PRs reviewed by Copilot |

## Out of scope

ESSP recommends several lagging metrics that **cannot** be derived from the GitHub APIs alone — they are intentionally **omitted** from this dashboard rather than shown with placeholder data:

- **Survey-based metrics** — Flow state experience, Copilot satisfaction, Engineering tooling satisfaction (ESSP Developer Happiness). Require human-input survey responses.
- **Code security & maintainability** — would need GitHub Advanced Security alert telemetry, which is not yet ingested into v3's schema.
- **Finance-based business outcomes** — AI leverage \$, engineering-expense-to-revenue, feature-engineering-expense ratio. Require payroll/revenue data.

When those data sources become available, new sections can be added without changing the existing panels.

panels.title: Deploys / Week (28d)
panels.description: Successful deployments per week, averaged over the last 28 days. Successful = the latest deployment_statuses row is state='success'.

targets.rawSql: WITH latest AS (SELECT DISTINCT ON (deployment_id) deployment_id, state FROM deployment_statuses ORDER BY deployment_id, created_at DESC) SELECT ROUND(COUNT(*)::numeric / 4.0, 1) AS value FROM deployments d JOIN latest l ON l.deployment_id = d.id WHERE l.state = 'success' AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.title: Successful Deploys (28d)
panels.description: Total deployments in the last 28 days whose latest status is 'success'.

targets.rawSql: WITH latest AS (SELECT DISTINCT ON (deployment_id) deployment_id, state FROM deployment_statuses ORDER BY deployment_id, created_at DESC) SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM deployments d JOIN latest l ON l.deployment_id = d.id WHERE l.state = 'success' AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.title: Deploy Success Rate
panels.description: Share of deployments in the last 28 days whose latest status is 'success'. Counts only deployments with a terminal status (success or failure).

targets.rawSql: WITH latest AS (SELECT DISTINCT ON (deployment_id) deployment_id, state FROM deployment_statuses ORDER BY deployment_id, created_at DESC) SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM((l.state='success')::int) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS value FROM deployments d JOIN latest l ON l.deployment_id = d.id WHERE l.state IN ('success','failure') AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.title: Weekly Deployments — Success vs Failure
panels.description: Deployments per week bucketed by deployments.created_at, split by latest deployment_statuses.state.

targets.rawSql: WITH latest AS (SELECT DISTINCT ON (deployment_id) deployment_id, state FROM deployment_statuses ORDER BY deployment_id, created_at DESC) SELECT date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time, SUM((l.state='success')::int) AS "Success", SUM((l.state='failure')::int) AS "Failure" FROM deployments d JOIN latest l ON l.deployment_id = d.id WHERE d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

options.content:
**ESSP definition:** *How often releases are deployed to production. Increase is good. Higher deployment frequency may enable rapid innovation and faster customer feedback cycles.* — [DORA quickcheck](https://dora.dev/quickcheck/)

**Calculation:**
- *Deploys / Week* = `COUNT(successful deployments in 28d) / 4`.
- A deployment is *successful* when its **latest** `deployment_statuses` row has `state='success'` (a deployment may go through `pending → in_progress → success` and we only care about the final state).
- *Deploy Success Rate* counts only deployments with a terminal status (`success` or `failure`); in-progress deployments are excluded.

**How to interpret:**

| Deploys / week | DORA band | Action |
|---|---|---|
| ≥ 7 (daily+) | Elite | Maintain — keep deployment automation healthy |
| 1 – 7 | High | Look for further automation opportunities |
| < 1 (weekly–monthly) | Medium | Investigate manual gates, batched releases |
| < 1 / month | Low | Significant friction — prioritize CI/CD investment |

**Caveat:** v3 does not filter by environment — deployments to staging, preview, or production all count. If you only want production, add an `AND d.environment = 'production'` clause.

panels.title: Lead Time for Changes — Learning Guide
Median Lead Time (hrs)
panels.description: P50 of (deployment created_at − PR merged_at) for PRs whose merge commit reached a successful production deployment. Pure DORA lead-time-for-changes.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(percentile_cont(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.created_at - pr.merged_at))/3600.0)::numeric, 1) AS value FROM pull_requests pr JOIN deployment_pr_links l ON l.pr_number = pr.number JOIN deployments d ON d.id = l.deployment_id JOIN deployment_statuses s ON s.deployment_id = d.id AND s.state = 'success' WHERE pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days' AND d.created_at >= pr.merged_at

panels.title: P90 Lead Time (hrs)
panels.description: P90 of (deployment created_at − PR merged_at). 1 in 10 changes take longer than this — surfaces the long tail.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(percentile_cont(0.9) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.created_at - pr.merged_at))/3600.0)::numeric, 1) AS value FROM pull_requests pr JOIN deployment_pr_links l ON l.pr_number = pr.number JOIN deployments d ON d.id = l.deployment_id JOIN deployment_statuses s ON s.deployment_id = d.id AND s.state = 'success' WHERE pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days' AND d.created_at >= pr.merged_at

panels.title: PRs Deployed (28d)
panels.description: Count of PRs that merged AND reached a successful deployment in the last 28 days. Volume context for the percentile stats.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pr.number) AS value FROM pull_requests pr JOIN deployment_pr_links l ON l.pr_number = pr.number JOIN deployments d ON d.id = l.deployment_id JOIN deployment_statuses s ON s.deployment_id = d.id AND s.state = 'success' WHERE pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.title: Lead Time over Time (P50 vs P90, hours)
panels.description: Weekly P50 and P90 of merge → successful deploy duration. Watch for divergence: if P90 grows while P50 stays flat, a slow tail is forming.

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time, ROUND(percentile_cont(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.created_at - pr.merged_at))/3600.0)::numeric, 2) AS "P50", ROUND(percentile_cont(0.9) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.created_at - pr.merged_at))/3600.0)::numeric, 2) AS "P90" FROM pull_requests pr JOIN deployment_pr_links l ON l.pr_number = pr.number JOIN deployments d ON d.id = l.deployment_id JOIN deployment_statuses s ON s.deployment_id = d.id AND s.state = 'success' WHERE pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days' AND d.created_at >= pr.merged_at GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Lead Time for Changes — Learning Guide

options.content:
**ESSP definition:** *The amount of time it takes a commit to get into production. Decrease is good. Shorter lead times may enable faster responses to market demands.* — [DORA quickcheck](https://dora.dev/quickcheck/)

**Why P50 and P90?**
- **P50 (median)** — the typical change. Half of all changes ship faster than this, half slower.
- **P90** — 90% of changes ship faster than this, 10% take longer. Surfaces the *systemic* slow tail without overreacting to a single freak event.
- DORA and ESSP both recommend reporting **both** because P50 alone can hide that 10% of work is stuck for days.

**Calculation:** `deployment.created_at − pull_requests.merged_at`, joined via `deployment_pr_links` (the bridge resolver matches deployment SHAs to PR `merge_commit_sha` or `head_sha`). Only deployments with a successful status are counted.

**How to interpret (DORA bands):**

| Median lead time | DORA band |
|---|---|
| < 1 day | Elite |
| 1 day – 1 week | High |
| 1 week – 1 month | Medium |
| > 1 month | Low |

**Difference vs the leading dashboards:** Dashboard 10 (`Pull Request Merge & Lead Time` section) reports *minutes-to-merge* (PR `created_at` → `merged_at`) — that is the **review** sub-component. This dashboard adds the **deploy** sub-component (`merged_at` → `deploy success`) to give the full DORA lead-time picture. Neither is captured by the Copilot Metrics API ([pull-request activity fields](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics#pull-request-activity-fields-api-only)) — those fields count *engagement events*, not durations.

panels.title: Change Failure Rate
panels.description: Share of successful deployments that have a coincident incident issue (label='incident', issue created_at within ±24h of deployment created_at).

targets.rawSql: WITH latest AS (SELECT DISTINCT ON (deployment_id) deployment_id, state FROM deployment_statuses ORDER BY deployment_id, created_at DESC), deploys AS (SELECT d.id, d.created_at FROM deployments d JOIN latest l ON l.deployment_id = d.id WHERE l.state = 'success' AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'), fails AS (SELECT DISTINCT dp.id FROM deploys dp JOIN issues i ON i.labels @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'::jsonb AND i.created_at BETWEEN dp.created_at - INTERVAL '24 hours' AND dp.created_at + INTERVAL '24 hours') SELECT ROUND(100.0 * (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fails) / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM deploys), 0), 1) AS value

panels.title: Total Incidents (28d)
panels.description: Count of issues opened in the last 28 days carrying the 'incident' label.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM issues WHERE labels @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'::jsonb AND created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.title: Deployment Rework Rate
panels.description: Share of deployments followed within 48h by another deployment to the same environment. Proxy for hotfix-style rework.

targets.rawSql: WITH d AS (SELECT id, environment, created_at FROM deployments WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days') SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM d d2 WHERE d2.environment = d.environment AND d2.id <> d.id AND d2.created_at > d.created_at AND d2.created_at <= d.created_at + INTERVAL '48 hours') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS value FROM d

panels.title: Change Failure Rate over Time (weekly)
panels.description: Weekly CFR % bucketed by deployment week.

targets.rawSql: WITH latest AS (SELECT DISTINCT ON (deployment_id) deployment_id, state FROM deployment_statuses ORDER BY deployment_id, created_at DESC), deploys AS (SELECT d.id, d.created_at FROM deployments d JOIN latest l ON l.deployment_id = d.id WHERE l.state = 'success' AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'), tagged AS (SELECT dp.id, dp.created_at, EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM issues i WHERE i.labels @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'::jsonb AND i.created_at BETWEEN dp.created_at - INTERVAL '24 hours' AND dp.created_at + INTERVAL '24 hours') AS failed FROM deploys dp) SELECT date_trunc('week', created_at) AS time, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(failed::int)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0), 1) AS "CFR %" FROM tagged GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

options.content:
**ESSP definition:** *The percentage of changes to production or released to users that result in degraded service. Decrease is good. Lower change failure rate may mean higher reliability and fewer disruptions for customers.* — [DORA quickcheck](https://dora.dev/quickcheck/)

**Calculation:**
- *CFR* — successful deployments with at least one `incident`-labeled issue created within ±24h of the deployment, divided by total successful deployments.
- *Rework Rate* — deployments followed within 48h by another deployment to the *same* environment. Useful as a proxy when teams don't label incidents but do hot-fix-redeploy.
- *Total Incidents* — raw `incident`-labeled issue count, for context. A high CFR with few incidents may simply mean issue labeling has lapsed.

**How to interpret (DORA bands):**

| CFR | DORA band | Action |
|---|---|---|
| 0 – 15% | Elite/High | Healthy — maintain |
| 16 – 30% | Medium | Investigate flaky tests, deploy gates |
| 31 – 45% | Low | Significant quality concern |
| > 45% | Critical | Stop and stabilize before adding velocity |

**⚠️ Caveat:** This metric **requires incidents to be labeled `incident`** in GitHub Issues. If your team uses a different label (e.g. `outage`, `sev1`), the CFR will read 0% — that's not an error, it's a labeling gap. Update the SQL `labels @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'::jsonb` clause to match your convention.

panels.title: Failed Deployment Recovery Time (MTTR) — Learning Guide
Median MTTR (hrs)
panels.description: P50 of recovery duration: for each failed deployment, the time until the next successful deployment to the same environment.

targets.rawSql: WITH latest AS (SELECT DISTINCT ON (deployment_id) deployment_id, state FROM deployment_statuses ORDER BY deployment_id, created_at DESC), labelled AS (SELECT d.id, d.environment, d.created_at, l.state FROM deployments d JOIN latest l ON l.deployment_id = d.id), recoveries AS (SELECT f.environment, f.created_at AS failed_at, (SELECT MIN(s.created_at) FROM labelled s WHERE s.environment = f.environment AND s.state = 'success' AND s.created_at > f.created_at) AS recovered_at FROM labelled f WHERE f.state = 'failure' AND f.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days') SELECT ROUND(percentile_cont(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (recovered_at - failed_at))/3600.0)::numeric, 1) AS value FROM recoveries WHERE recovered_at IS NOT NULL

panels.title: P90 MTTR (hrs)
panels.description: P90 of recovery duration. 1 in 10 incidents take longer than this to resolve — captures worst-case downtime.

targets.rawSql: WITH latest AS (SELECT DISTINCT ON (deployment_id) deployment_id, state FROM deployment_statuses ORDER BY deployment_id, created_at DESC), labelled AS (SELECT d.id, d.environment, d.created_at, l.state FROM deployments d JOIN latest l ON l.deployment_id = d.id), recoveries AS (SELECT f.environment, f.created_at AS failed_at, (SELECT MIN(s.created_at) FROM labelled s WHERE s.environment = f.environment AND s.state = 'success' AND s.created_at > f.created_at) AS recovered_at FROM labelled f WHERE f.state = 'failure' AND f.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days') SELECT ROUND(percentile_cont(0.9) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (recovered_at - failed_at))/3600.0)::numeric, 1) AS value FROM recoveries WHERE recovered_at IS NOT NULL

panels.title: Open Incidents
panels.description: Count of issues currently in state='open' carrying the 'incident' label (all-time — open until closed).

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM issues WHERE state = 'open' AND labels @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'::jsonb

panels.title: MTTR over Time (P50 vs P90, hours)
panels.description: Weekly P50 and P90 of failure → next success duration, bucketed by the failure week.

targets.rawSql: WITH latest AS (SELECT DISTINCT ON (deployment_id) deployment_id, state FROM deployment_statuses ORDER BY deployment_id, created_at DESC), labelled AS (SELECT d.id, d.environment, d.created_at, l.state FROM deployments d JOIN latest l ON l.deployment_id = d.id), recoveries AS (SELECT f.environment, f.created_at AS failed_at, (SELECT MIN(s.created_at) FROM labelled s WHERE s.environment = f.environment AND s.state = 'success' AND s.created_at > f.created_at) AS recovered_at FROM labelled f WHERE f.state = 'failure' AND f.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days') SELECT date_trunc('week', failed_at) AS time, ROUND(percentile_cont(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (recovered_at - failed_at))/3600.0)::numeric, 2) AS "P50", ROUND(percentile_cont(0.9) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (recovered_at - failed_at))/3600.0)::numeric, 2) AS "P90" FROM recoveries WHERE recovered_at IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Failed Deployment Recovery Time — Learning Guide

options.content:
**ESSP definition:** *How long it takes an organization to recover from a failure in production. Decrease is good. Faster recovery from deployment failures may mean reduced downtime and maintains customer trust.* — [DORA quickcheck](https://dora.dev/quickcheck/)

**Calculation:** For each deployment whose latest status is `failure`, find the **next** deployment to the *same* environment whose latest status is `success`. Recovery duration = `next.created_at − failure.created_at`. The stats are P50 and P90 of those durations over the last 28 days; the timeseries buckets by the failure week.

**How to interpret (DORA bands):**

| Median MTTR | DORA band |
|---|---|
| < 1 hour | Elite |
| 1 – 24 hours | High |
| 1 day – 1 week | Medium |
| > 1 week | Low |

**Open Incidents** — `issues` with `state='open'` and label `incident`. Complements MTTR by showing the *current* unresolved load. MTTR alone hides incidents that are still in progress (no `recovered_at` yet → excluded from the percentile calc).

**Caveats:**
- Recovery is detected via the *next deployment* to the same environment. If recovery happens via a hotfix targeting a different environment, this metric undercounts.
- Failures that have not yet recovered are excluded from the percentile (they have no end timestamp). Watch the **Open Incidents** stat alongside MTTR for a complete picture.

panels.title: PRs Merged per Active Developer — Learning Guide
PRs / Active Dev (28d)
panels.description: pull_requests.total_merged ÷ monthly_active_users from the enterprise Copilot Metrics API. ESSP Activity metric — total throughput adjusted for headcount.

targets.rawSql: WITH agg AS (SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_merged')::int) AS merged, MAX(monthly_active_users) AS mau FROM copilot_enterprise_daily WHERE day >= (SELECT MAX(day) - INTERVAL '27 days' FROM copilot_enterprise_daily)) SELECT ROUND(merged::numeric / NULLIF(mau, 0), 2) AS value FROM agg

panels.title: Monthly Active Users (28d)
panels.description: Peak monthly_active_users from the enterprise Copilot Metrics API over the last 28 days — the denominator of PRs / Active Dev.

targets.rawSql: SELECT MAX(monthly_active_users) AS value FROM copilot_enterprise_daily WHERE day >= (SELECT MAX(day) - INTERVAL '27 days' FROM copilot_enterprise_daily)

panels.title: PRs / Active Dev / Week
panels.description: PRs Merged ÷ Active Devs ÷ 4 — weekly throughput per developer.

targets.rawSql: WITH agg AS (SELECT SUM((pull_requests->>'total_merged')::int) AS merged, MAX(monthly_active_users) AS mau FROM copilot_enterprise_daily WHERE day >= (SELECT MAX(day) - INTERVAL '27 days' FROM copilot_enterprise_daily)) SELECT ROUND(merged::numeric / NULLIF(mau, 0) / 4.0, 2) AS value FROM agg

panels.title: PRs Merged per Active Developer — Daily
panels.description: Daily ratio of (pull_requests.total_merged) to (monthly_active_users) from the enterprise Copilot Metrics API.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, ROUND((pull_requests->>'total_merged')::numeric / NULLIF(monthly_active_users, 0), 3) AS "PRs Merged / Active Dev" FROM copilot_enterprise_daily WHERE day >= (SELECT MAX(day) - INTERVAL '27 days' FROM copilot_enterprise_daily) ORDER BY day

panels.title: PRs Merged per Active Developer — Learning Guide

options.content:
**ESSP definition:** *Number of pull requests successfully merged divided by total developers. Increase is good. Higher PR merge rates per developer may indicate effective collaboration and accelerated delivery.*

**API source (lagging — outcome of merged work):**
- `pull_requests.total_merged` — from the enterprise [Copilot Metrics API](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-usage-metrics/copilot-usage-metrics#pull-request-activity-fields-api-only) (`/enterprises/{enterprise}/copilot/metrics/reports/enterprise-28-day/latest`).
- `monthly_active_users` — same API, `day_totals[].monthly_active_users` field.

Stored in the `copilot_enterprise_daily` table.

**Calculation:** `SUM(pull_requests->>'total_merged') / MAX(monthly_active_users)` over 28 days. The weekly variant divides by 4.

**⚠️ ESSP guidance — read carefully:**

> *"Focus on total PRs rather than calculating average for an individual and then calculating the mean. GitHub recommends taking particular care in the calculation of this metric. **It should not be used to compare engineers to one another.** Instead, the metric's purpose is to provide a measure of output adjusted for the number of engineers working within a team or organization."*

This dashboard intentionally aggregates at the **enterprise** level only — there is no per-user breakdown here. For per-user activity, use the [👤 Per-User Copilot Metrics](/d/edu-per-user-copilot) dashboard.

**Difference from the leading dashboard (10):** Dashboard 10's *Pull Request Merge & Lead Time* section shows raw `total_merged` and `total_merged_created_by_copilot` counts as **adoption** signals. This panel reframes the same numerator as a **productivity outcome ratio** — adjusted for active-developer headcount as the ESSP playbook prescribes.
